timeline.title: Coach Tiffany Begins Teaching
timeline.desc: Coach Carroll graduates from East Carolina University and begins teaching elementary physical education, where she is introduced to school jump rope teams and the larger world of the sport.

timeline.title: School Teams and Coaching Roots
timeline.desc: While teaching in public elementary school, Coach Tiffany coaches jump rope teams for 4th and 5th graders and builds the foundation for her coaching philosophy.

timeline.title: Driveway Practice and Community Clubs
timeline.desc: As a stay at home mom, Coach Tiffany keeps jumping with her own children and helps lead small community jump rope clubs.

timeline.title: COA SKIP ITS Formed
timeline.desc: Coach Tiffany begins teaching PE at Cathedral Oak Academy. COA SKIP ITS forms as a school performance team for students in grades 3 through 10, and Coach Olivia joins as assistant coach.

timeline.title: Mentorship Program Developed
timeline.desc: Coach David Ashey joins the coaching family and the mentorship program is developed to help older athletes invest in younger teammates.

timeline.title: Salem Jump Rope Established
timeline.desc: Coach David Carroll joins as the conditioning coach to assist Salem Jump Rope as a performance based jump rope team built around faith, skill, character, and leadership.

mentorValues.title: Leadership
mentorValues.desc: Mentors model responsibility, service, coachability, and Christlike leadership at practices and team events.

mentorValues.title: Accountability
mentorValues.desc: Mentors are trained by coaches and held to high standards of conduct, consistency, and encouragement.

mentorValues.title: Confidence
mentorValues.desc: Younger athletes receive one on one skill support and encouragement so they can attempt hard things with courage.

mentorValues.title: Service
mentorValues.desc: Mentorship teaches older athletes to invest in someone else's growth and continue the cycle of encouragement.

mentorValues.title: Teamwork
mentorValues.desc: Every athlete is reminded that the team is stronger when everyone feels valued, supported, and included.

coaches.name: Coach Tiffany Carroll
coaches.bio: Coach Carroll graduated from East Carolina University in 2008 with a degree in Exercise and Sport Science with a concentration in Physical Education. Her jump rope journey began through school teams and grew into a passion for helping students develop skill, confidence, character, leadership, and lasting friendships.
coaches.experience: Physical education teacher, school jump rope coach, Salem Jump Rope founder
coaches.focus: "Jump rope is an opportunity to build character, leadership, friendship, and positive impact."

coaches.name: Coach David Carroll
coaches.bio: Coach David brings a lifelong love of sports and a service minded background as a police officer, state law enforcement ranger, firefighter engineer, and EMT. He coaches alongside Coach Tiffany and helps athletes grow in strength, coordination, creativity, confidence, and teamwork.
coaches.experience: NASM CERTIFIED PERSONAL TRAINER, Nutrition Coach & Youth Exercise Specialist
coaches.focus: "Hard work, teamwork, and having fun can shape healthy, confident young people."

chatbotTopics.label: Tryout Info
chatbotTopics.response: Tryouts are scheduled for Thursday, September 10, 2026 from 3:30 PM to 4:45 PM at College Park Baptist Church. They are open to boys and girls in grades 3 through 12. No payment is collected online.

chatbotTopics.label: Contact the Team
chatbotTopics.response: The team email will be added after the domain is secured. You can also fill out the Contact form on our Contact page and we typically respond within 48 hours.

chatbotTopics.label: Book a Performance
chatbotTopics.response: We perform at schools, churches, halftime shows, community events, camps, and more! Visit our Book Us page and fill out the Performance Request Form. We'll follow up within 5 business days.

chatbotTopics.label: Sponsorship

chatbotTopics.label: Store / Merch

chatbotTopics.label: Team Access
chatbotTopics.response: Team Access can use one shared team username and password after the site is hosted. The password should be set through the website host, not inside the public page files.

tutorials.name: Basic Bounce
tutorials.desc: The foundation of all jump rope skills. Master your timing and rhythm with this entry level technique.

tutorials.name: Alternate Foot Step
tutorials.desc: Jump rope like you're running in place. Improves coordination and foot speed.

tutorials.name: Side Swing
tutorials.desc: A foundational trick that adds style and sets up more complex combinations.

tutorials.name: Criss Cross
tutorials.desc: Cross your arms in front and jump through. This is a classic crowd pleaser skill.

tutorials.name: Double Under
tutorials.desc: Two rotations of the rope for every one jump. Builds power, timing, and coordination.

tutorials.name: Speed Step
tutorials.desc: Fast paced alternating foot jumps. Great for speed and endurance development.

tutorials.name: Double Dutch Entry
tutorials.desc: Master the timing to enter two turning ropes. This is one of the most exciting skills in the sport.

tutorials.name: Partner Skills
tutorials.desc: Synchronized partner jumping requiring communication, rhythm, and trust.

tryoutSkills: Double unders, as many as possible
Jump 10x your age on single bounce, or as many as you can
Any additional skills you want to show

workouts.title: First Jump Workout
workouts.warmup: 5 min dynamic stretching, arm circles, jog in place
workouts.skills: 3 min basic bounce, 3 min alternate foot, rest 30 sec between

workouts.title: Skill Builder Session
workouts.warmup: 5 min jump activation: basic bounce, side swings
workouts.skills: Criss cross practice 5 min, double under attempts 5 min
workouts.conditioning: 5 rounds: 45 sec jump, 15 sec rest + 2 min speed steps

workouts.title: Power & Speed Session
workouts.warmup: 6 min full warmup circuit: jump, shuffle, side swings
workouts.skills: Double unders 8 min, Double Dutch timing 10 min
workouts.conditioning: Tabata: 8 rounds 20 sec max speed / 10 sec rest

workouts.title: Team Endurance Builder
workouts.warmup: 8 min team warmup: group jump in, sync drills
workouts.skills: Group routine practice, Double Dutch rotations
workouts.conditioning: 3 rounds of: 90 sec team jump relay, 30 sec recovery

faq.q: How do I register for a clinic?
faq.a: Use the Clinic Interest Form on our Clinic page to express your interest. A team representative will follow up with registration details, dates, and any fees involved.

faq.q: What age groups can try out for the team?
faq.a: Salem Jump Rope tryouts are open to boys and girls in grades 3 through 12.

faq.q: Do kids need prior jump rope experience to attend a clinic?
faq.a: No! Clinics are open to all experience levels, including complete beginners. Our coaching staff works with each student at their level.

faq.q: How can we book Salem Jump Rope for our event?
faq.a: Use the Performance Request Form on our Book Us page. We perform at schools, churches, community events, halftime shows, camps, and more. A team representative will follow up within 5 business days.

faq.q: Is there a cost to try out?
faq.a: Tryout interest is free to express through our online form. Any associated fees for the tryout session or team participation will be communicated directly by the coaching staff.
The Salem Jump Rope online store will open later. Sign up for notifications on the Store page and we will let you know when gear is available.

faq.q: How can my business sponsor the team?
faq.a: We have multiple sponsorship levels available: Ace Sponsor, King Sponsor, Queen Sponsor, Jack Sponsor, and Friend Sponsor. Fill out the Sponsorship Interest Form on our Sponsorship page and we'll be in touch.

faq.q: Where do clinics and practices take place?
faq.a: The Mini Fall Clinic is scheduled for River Oaks Community Church, and the September 10, 2026 tryout is scheduled for College Park Baptist Church. Practice locations and any changes will be confirmed by the coaching staff.

fitnessCards.title: 10 Min Beginner Jump Rope Workout
fitnessCards.desc: Perfect for first timers or warmup sessions. Basic bounce intervals with rest periods designed to build the jump rope habit.

fitnessCards.title: 20 Min Conditioning Workout
fitnessCards.desc: A full conditioning session combining speed steps, double unders, and basic tricks with timed intervals.

fitnessCards.title: Speed & Agility Workout
fitnessCards.desc: High intensity agility training using jump rope drills that build foot speed, reaction time, and athletic coordination.

fitnessCards.title: Family Jump Challenge
fitnessCards.desc: A fun family friendly workout designed for all ages. Rotate through basic skills together. No experience required.

fitnessCards.title: Team Endurance Builder
fitnessCards.desc: Long form endurance conditioning designed for competitive athletes. Builds cardiovascular base and mental toughness.
